sourceMovie.title: Extraction
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Extraction for the same reason it broke through in 2020: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Sam Hargrave keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Expendables
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Extraction clicked for you, The Expendables (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Extraction 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Extraction clicked for you, Extraction 2 (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Extraction?
faq.answer: Try The Expendables, Extraction 2, Ransom—each shares DNA with Extraction while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Extraction?
faq.answer: Extraction (2020) is best described as Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Extraction part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Extraction Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
